key_topics: Number: ordering and rounding decimals to three decimal places
Percentages: calculating percentage increase and decrease
Ratio and proportion: simplifying ratios, direct proportion
Algebra: using letters to represent unknowns, writing expressions
Solving linear equations with one unknown
Sequences: nth term (linear sequences)
Geometry: angles at a point, on a straight line, in a triangle, in a quadrilateral
Properties of circles: radius, diameter, circumference, area
Volume of cuboids and prisms
Transformations: scale factor for enlargements
Data: mean, median, mode, range from frequency tables
Probability: probability scale 0 to 1, mutually exclusive events
competences: Solve one-step and two-step linear equations
Calculate circumference and area of circles
Use the probability scale and calculate simple probabilities
Find the nth term of a linear sequence
typical_mistakes: Calculating percentage decrease by subtraction rather than multiplying by the complement
Confusing circumference and area formulae for circles
Errors solving equations with unknowns on both sides
Mistaking probability of 0 as impossible vs. just very unlikely

key_topics: Reading: sustained engagement with novels and extended non-fiction
Critical analysis: evaluating effectiveness of language choices
Comparing writers' perspectives and techniques
Reading for different purposes: revision strategies, skimming, scanning
Writing: sophisticated narrative with multiple narrative threads
Discursive writing: synthesising multiple sources
Writing for a specific audience and purpose (letter, report, speech, review)
Grammar: complex sentence structures, varied punctuation
Editing and proofreading own work systematically
Spelling: morphology, etymology, common exceptions
Presentation and speaking: debate, formal speech, interview
competences: Critically evaluate writers' language choices with evidence
Write extended pieces in a range of forms for specific audiences
Synthesise information from multiple sources in writing
Edit and improve own writing for effect and accuracy
typical_mistakes: Quoting without explaining the effect of the language
Not adapting writing style to audience and purpose
Failing to use a range of sentence structures (over-reliance on complex sentences)

key_topics: Cells: plant and animal cells — structure and function
Micro-organisms: their role in decomposition and disease
Adaptation and evolution: Darwin's theory, natural selection, evidence
Electricity: series and parallel circuits, resistance
Light: refraction, the eye, optical illusions
Sound: how sound is heard, hearing and the ear
Earth and beyond: moon phases, eclipses, satellites
Chemistry: chemical reactions vs. physical changes, acids and alkalis
competences: Compare plant and animal cells at a structural level
Analyse series and parallel circuits, predict effects of changes
Describe refraction and how the eye works
typical_mistakes: Thinking evolution is directional (toward humans as the goal)
Confusing series and parallel circuit behaviour
